title: Flip worker singleton to multi-worker default

proposal.approval_evidence: Proposal confirmed via inline approval. Problem statement derived from live investigation of 5-session multi-worker setup showing ghost workers and false degraded diagnostics.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery findings confirmed: worker_role is purely diagnostic, lock classification is dead code in production, health monitor restart is already singleton-agnostic. No blockers.

design.approval_evidence: Design complete. Validator CAUTION resolved inline — spec scope expanded to include rq-advcfg01.2 and rq-temporalConcurrentLoad01. No architectural changes, no user-value tradeoffs, no contract-compromise risk.

planning.approval_evidence: Plan approved by user. 5 tasks: default flip, diagnostic fix, spec relaxation, test/doc updates, full suite verification.

execution.approval_evidence: All 5 tasks complete. pnpm run check clean. 2916/2917 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in adv-tron-assets.test.ts unrelated to this change).

acceptance.approval_evidence: All 12 contract items pass/respected. 2916/2917 tests pass (1 pre-existing). Executive summary persisted.

release.approval_evidence: User sign-off for archive. All gates complete, contract review clean, tests green.

items.text: **AC1**: With default config (no `worker_singleton_enforce` in project.json), two concurrent OpenCode sessions on the same project each spawn their own worker and both report `serviceable` status — no session reports "degraded" or "not alive"
**AC2**: With `worker_singleton_enforce: true` in project.json, second session takes client role and does not spawn a worker — behavior identical to current production
**AC3**: `adv_temporal_diagnose` does not recommend "run adv_temporal_restart" when worker_alive=false but queue has active server-side pollers
**AC4**: All tests pass (`pnpm test`, `pnpm run check`)
**AC5**: `docs/specs/advance-meta.md` shows `rq-workerSingleton01` as SHOULD with opt-in description
**C1**: `worker-lock.ts` and `worker-heartbeat.ts` modules must remain importable and functional — no deletion
**C2**: No changes to Temporal workflow definitions, signal contracts, or query schemas
**C3**: Existing project.json files with explicit `worker_singleton_enforce: true` must continue to work identically
**C4**: No workflow state migration — this is a plugin-init-time behavioral change only
**DONT1**: Do not delete or stub out worker-lock or worker-heartbeat modules
**DONT2**: Do not change the worker child process spawn mechanism (createMultiWorker / createOutOfProcessWorker)
**DONT3**: Do not introduce new feature flags — reuse the existing `worker_singleton_enforce` with flipped default

rows.evidence: Default flipped to false in types/project.ts. Existing fast path (plugin-init.ts:92) already handles singleton=false correctly. Multi-session test coverage via plugin-init.worker-singleton.test.ts with workerSingletonEnforce: false.
Existing tests with workerSingletonEnforce: true still pass (plugin-init.worker-singleton.test.ts lines 35-108). Lock acquisition path unchanged. C3 constraint verified.

New test in temporal-ops.test.ts: when worker_alive=false but probeTaskQueuePollers returns fresh, recommendation says 'serviceable via peer workers' not 'run adv_temporal_restart'. 8 temporal-ops tests pass.
pnpm run check: clean (typecheck+lint+format). pnpm test: 2916/2917 pass. 1 pre-existing failure (adv-tron-assets.test.ts) confirmed on main.
docs/specs/advance-meta.md: rq-workerSingleton01 changed to SHOULD with opt-in preamble, rq-advcfg01.2 updated to 'defaults false', rq-temporalConcurrentLoad01 scoped to singleton=true.
worker-lock.ts and worker-heartbeat.ts not modified. No imports removed from any file.
No changes to temporal/workflows.ts, contracts.ts, or any signal/query definitions.
withStabilityFeatureDefaults preserves explicit true values via ternary. Existing tests with workerSingletonEnforce: true pass.
No migration code. Change is plugin-init-time only. No workflow state touched.
worker-lock.ts and worker-heartbeat.ts untouched. No stubs or deletions.
Reused existing worker_singleton_enforce flag. No new flags introduced.

acceptanceCriteria: **AC1**: With default config (no `worker_singleton_enforce` in project.json), two concurrent OpenCode sessions on the same project each spawn their own worker and both report `serviceable` status — no session reports "degraded" or "not alive"
**AC2**: With `worker_singleton_enforce: true` in project.json, second session takes client role and does not spawn a worker — behavior identical to current production
**AC3**: `adv_temporal_diagnose` does not recommend "run adv_temporal_restart" when worker_alive=false but queue has active server-side pollers
**AC4**: All tests pass (`pnpm test`, `pnpm run check`)
**AC5**: `docs/specs/advance-meta.md` shows `rq-workerSingleton01` as SHOULD with opt-in description
